title: Engine, Tasks & Workflows Functional

acceptance_criteria_testability.rationale: All ACs testable. Engine health metrics verifiable via API. Queue size testable. Task progress verifiable in UI. Workflow progress verifiable. Color coding testable. Auto-refresh verifiable in DevTools.

no_invented_features.rationale: Every requirement traces to explicit AC. Engine/Tasks/Workflows are existing systems. No invented features — just surface existing data. Status color mapping is standard practice.

pattern_consistency.rationale: Follows Hook → useQuery → Component pattern from 3.10, 3.11, 3.12. Three separate hooks (useEngine, useTasks, useWorkflows). Consistent with other stories.

brownfield_context_respected.rationale: Updates existing dashboard components. Uses existing Engine/Task/Workflow systems (doesn't reinvent). Respects system architecture and existing patterns.

real_data_emphasis.rationale: All data from real Engine/Task/Workflow systems (no mocks). Testing emphasizes real system data. Statistics calculated from actual queue state.

backend_frontend_scope_clarity.rationale: Backend scope: 3 services + 3 endpoints. Frontend scope: 3 hooks + updated components. Clean separation. Slightly larger scope than 3.10-3.12 but well-defined.

reasoning: Story 3.13 is COMPLEX (18/25 pts) but well-scoped. Specification is detailed with clear implementation roadmap. Largest story in Wave 5. CRITICAL RECOMMENDATION: Consider splitting into 3.13a (Engine only, 5-8 pts) and 3.13b (Tasks+Workflows, 8-13 pts) to improve velocity and reduce per-story scope. As written, it's approvable but represents significant work. No revisions needed if team capacity allows single large story.
critical_notes: COMPLEX story (18/25 pts) — largest in Wave 5
Depends on 3.11 approval for pattern consistency
Performance-critical: 10-second polling must not overload API
ready_for_dev_reason: Depends on 3.11 approval AND recommend splitting story first
dev_notes: If keeping as single story: break into 3 phases (Engine, Tasks, Workflows)
Start with Engine service (simplest, good foundation)
Then Workflows service (requires workflow state understanding)
Test each endpoint with curl before frontend integration
Implement error boundaries for graceful API failure handling
Use staleTime=9s with 10s refetchInterval to prevent redundant fetches
Color coding: verify accessibility (text + color, not color-only)
Progress calculation: test with edge cases (0%, 100%, partial)
potential_blockers: Engine/Task/Workflow data sources must be available and queryable
Progress calculation logic may require system knowledge
Performance: 10s polling on 3 endpoints × dashboard instances may be heavy
Status enum consistency (what values are valid?)

suggested_split.title: Engine Status Real-Time
suggested_split.scope: Engine health card only (worker pool, queue size, CPU/memory/uptime)

suggested_split.title: Tasks & Workflows Functional
suggested_split.scope: Tasks card + Workflows card (lists, stats, progress tracking)
